codeReview.summary: The fix broadens the portability detector to catch Linux home/workspace roots and backslash-style Windows drive paths, and the current parity-adjacent docs are clean. However, the detector still misses forward-slash Windows drive absolute paths, so the docs contract remains narrower than the generic machine-local absolute-path rejection required by the prior blocker.

issues.description: `contains_machine_local_absolute_path` only recognizes drive-letter Windows paths when the separator after `X:` is `\`. Forward-slash forms such as `D:/a/openai-agents-rust/openai-agents-rust/docs/BEHAVIOR_PARITY.md` still return false, so the docs contract can miss machine-local absolute paths that use Windows drive letters with `/`. The regression added in this fix only covers the backslash variant (`D:\a\...`), leaving the original blocker only partially resolved.

The portability detector still accepts forward-slash Windows drive absolute paths like `D:/a/...`, so it does not yet reject generic machine-local absolute paths across the common Windows forms this audit is meant to guard against.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared `docs_audit` command still omits `docs_contract`, even though the docs portability/readme harness is part of the audit surface for VAL-AUDIT-003 and VAL-AUDIT-004.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/services.yaml:7-8 defines `docs_audit` as `cargo test -q -p openai-agents --test behavior_parity --test root_export_parity` and keeps `docs_contract` as a separate command, so the named audit entrypoint does not run the portability harness this feature modified.

summary: Reviewed the fix transcript skeleton, both the original and fix diffs, and the current repo state. The docs are currently clean and the detector now covers several non-macOS path shapes, but it still misses forward-slash Windows drive absolute paths, so the prior blocker is not fully closed.
